content:
First, note that if \(\deg f=d\), then as \(x\to\infty\) the left‐hand side
\[
f(x-1)+f(x)+f(x+1)\sim 3a\,x^d,
\]
where \(a\) is the leading coefficient of \(f\), while the right‐hand side
\[
\frac{f(x)^2}{2013\,x}\sim \frac{a^2x^{2d}}{2013x}
=\frac{a^2}{2013}\,x^{2d-1}.
\]
For these to match as polynomials we need \(2d-1=d\), so \(d=1\).  Hence
\[
f(x)=Ax+B,\quad A\neq0.
\]
Plugging into the equation gives
\[
(A(x-1)+B)+(Ax+B)+(A(x+1)+B)
=3Ax+3B
\]
on the left, while on the right
\[
\frac{(Ax+B)^2}{2013x}
=\frac{A^2x^2+2ABx+B^2}{2013x}
=\frac{A^2}{2013}\,x+\frac{2AB}{2013}+\frac{B^2}{2013x}.
\]
Since these agree for all \(x\ne0\), the term in \(1/x\) must vanish, forcing
\(B^2=0\), hence \(B=0\).  Equating the \(x\)-coefficients then gives
\[
3A=\frac{A^2}{2013}\;\Longrightarrow\;A(A-3\cdot2013)=0,
\]
and \(A\neq0\) whence \(A=3\cdot2013=6039\).  Thus the unique nonconstant
solution is
\[
f(x)=6039\,x,
\]
and so
\[
f(1)=6039.
\]
Hence the sum of all possible values of \(f(1)\) is \(\boxed{6039}\).

message.content:
First, note that if \(\deg f=d\), then as \(x\to\infty\) the left‐hand side
\[
f(x-1)+f(x)+f(x+1)\sim 3a\,x^d,
\]
where \(a\) is the leading coefficient of \(f\), while the right‐hand side
\[
\frac{f(x)^2}{2013\,x}\sim \frac{a^2x^{2d}}{2013x}
=\frac{a^2}{2013}\,x^{2d-1}.
\]
For these to match as polynomials we need \(2d-1=d\), so \(d=1\).  Hence
\[
f(x)=Ax+B,\quad A\neq0.
\]
Plugging into the equation gives
\[
(A(x-1)+B)+(Ax+B)+(A(x+1)+B)
=3Ax+3B
\]
on the left, while on the right
\[
\frac{(Ax+B)^2}{2013x}
=\frac{A^2x^2+2ABx+B^2}{2013x}
=\frac{A^2}{2013}\,x+\frac{2AB}{2013}+\frac{B^2}{2013x}.
\]
Since these agree for all \(x\ne0\), the term in \(1/x\) must vanish, forcing
\(B^2=0\), hence \(B=0\).  Equating the \(x\)-coefficients then gives
\[
3A=\frac{A^2}{2013}\;\Longrightarrow\;A(A-3\cdot2013)=0,
\]
and \(A\neq0\) whence \(A=3\cdot2013=6039\).  Thus the unique nonconstant
solution is
\[
f(x)=6039\,x,
\]
and so
\[
f(1)=6039.
\]
Hence the sum of all possible values of \(f(1)\) is \(\boxed{6039}\).
